itinerary.mapNote: Leave Oviedo cathedral. The Original Way begins in the ancient Asturian capital. 300m.
Rolling Asturian countryside. Green hills and village churches. 400m.
Altitude increases. Atlantic Spain shifting toward mountain Spain. 600m.
Deep Asturian valleys. Ancient Roman road sections. 700m.

itinerary.route: Pola de Allande to La Mesa
itinerary.mapNote: Into the high Cantabrian range. The toughest section begins. 1000m.
Cross into Galicia. Language changes, cuisine changes. 900m.
Descent to Roman Lugo — complete Roman walls intact. 600m.
Junction with the Camino Francés. Pulpo a la gallega capital. 450m.
Final hill before Santiago. First cathedral glimpse. 350m.

itinerary.route: Monte do Gozo to Santiago de Compostela
itinerary.mapNote: Arrive at the Cathedral. Pilgrim Mass. Abrazo of Saint James. 260m.

waypoints.name: Oviedo Cathedral
waypoints.note: The original starting point. King Alfonso II first walked this in 830 AD.

waypoints.name: Cruz de los Caminos
waypoints.note: Highest point on the Primitivo. Ancient Celtic cross at the summit.

waypoints.name: Lugo Roman Walls
waypoints.note: UNESCO World Heritage Roman walls. Only complete Roman circuit in the world.

waypoints.name: Santiago Cathedral
waypoints.note: The final destination. Gold baroque facade. Botafumeiro incense ceremony.
